(July 12, 1946 – March 31, 2025) was an American actress who mainly appeared on television throughout the 1970s. A native of [Reading, Pennsylvania](/wiki/Reading,_Pennsylvania "Reading, Pennsylvania"), Allen studied at the [Pasadena Playhouse](/wiki/Pasadena_Playhouse "Pasadena Playhouse") before appearing in her first screen role on the series *[O'Hara, U.S. Treasury](/wiki/O%27Hara,_U.S._Treasury "O'Hara, U.S. Treasury")* in 1971. She went on to appear in numerous television series in the ensuing years, including recurring appearances on *[The Waltons](/wiki/The_Waltons "The Waltons")*, *[Gunsmoke](/wiki/Gunsmoke "Gunsmoke")*, and *[Ironside](/wiki/Ironside_(1967_TV_series) "Ironside (1967 TV series)")*.[[1]](#cite_note-TVGuide-1)

**Sian Barbara Allen** (July 12, 1946 – March 31, 2025) was an American actress who mainly appeared on television throughout the 1970s. A native of [Reading, Pennsylvania](/wiki/Reading,_Pennsylvania "Reading, Pennsylvania"), Allen studied at the [Pasadena Playhouse](/wiki/Pasadena_Playhouse "Pasadena Playhouse") before appearing in her first screen role on the series *[O'Hara, U.S. Treasury](/wiki/O%27Hara,_U.S._Treasury "O'Hara, U.S. Treasury")* in 1971. She went on to appear in numerous television series in the ensuing years, including recurring appearances on *[The Waltons](/wiki/The_Waltons "The Waltons")*, *[Gunsmoke](/wiki/Gunsmoke "Gunsmoke")*, and *[Ironside](/wiki/Ironside_(1967_TV_series) "Ironside (1967 TV series)")*.[[1]](#cite_note-TVGuide-1)

She starred in several television films, including *[Scream, Pretty Peggy](/wiki/Scream,_Pretty_Peggy "Scream, Pretty Peggy")* (1973) and *[The Lindbergh Kidnapping Case](/wiki/The_Lindbergh_Kidnapping_Case "The Lindbergh Kidnapping Case")* (1976). In addition to her television credits, Allen starred in two feature films: the thriller *[You'll Like My Mother](/wiki/You%27ll_Like_My_Mother "You'll Like My Mother")* (1972) and the Western *[Billy Two Hats](/wiki/Billy_Two_Hats "Billy Two Hats")* (1974). For her performance in *You'll Like My Mother*, Allen was nominated for a [Golden Globe Award](/wiki/Golden_Globe_Awards "Golden Globe Awards") for [New Star of the Year](/wiki/Golden_Globe_Award_for_New_Star_of_the_Year_%E2%80%93_Actress "Golden Globe Award for New Star of the Year – Actress").

## Early life and education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sian_Barbara_Allen&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life and education")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c3/James_Garner_Rockford_Files_1974.JPG/250px-James_Garner_Rockford_Files_1974.JPG)](/wiki/File:James_Garner_Rockford_Files_1974.JPG)

With [James Garner](/wiki/James_Garner "James Garner") and David Morick in the 1974 *[The Rockford Files](/wiki/The_Rockford_Files "The Rockford Files")* episode "Tall Woman in Red Wagon"

Allen was born on July 12, 1946.[[2]](#cite_note-2) She studied at the Pasadena Playhouse in [Pasadena, California](/wiki/Pasadena,_California "Pasadena, California"), between 1964 and 1965.[[3]](#cite_note-Mor_Rec-3)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sian_Barbara_Allen&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

Allen appeared in the horror film *[You'll Like My Mother](/wiki/You%27ll_Like_My_Mother "You'll Like My Mother")* (1972), starring [Patty Duke](/wiki/Patty_Duke "Patty Duke"), [Rosemary Murphy](/wiki/Rosemary_Murphy "Rosemary Murphy") and [Richard Thomas](/wiki/Richard_Thomas_(actor) "Richard Thomas (actor)").[[4]](#cite_note-4) For this role, Allen was nominated for the 1973 [Golden Globe Award](/wiki/Golden_Globe_Awards "Golden Globe Awards") as Most Promising New Actress.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

She later appeared in two episodes of the popular TV series *[The Waltons](/wiki/The_Waltons "The Waltons")* (1973) as Jenny Pendleton, an early love interest of John-Boy Walton (reuniting her with Thomas, who played John-Boy and with whom she had appeared in *You'll Like My Mother* the previous year).[[1]](#cite_note-TVGuide-1) Allen and Thomas were themselves described as "together these days", and Thomas wanted Allen to play the Pendleton role.[[3]](#cite_note-Mor_Rec-3)[[6]](#cite_note-6) She played the title role of a mansion's housekeeper in the 1973 [ABC Movie of the Week](/wiki/ABC_Movie_of_the_Week "ABC Movie of the Week"), *[Scream, Pretty Peggy](/wiki/Scream,_Pretty_Peggy "Scream, Pretty Peggy")*, which starred [Bette Davis](/wiki/Bette_Davis "Bette Davis").[[7]](#cite_note-7)

Allen appeared on [television series](/wiki/Television_show#series "Television show") and miniseries such as *[Captains and the Kings](/wiki/Captains_and_the_Kings "Captains and the Kings")*; *[The Incredible Hulk](/wiki/The_Incredible_Hulk_(1978_TV_series) "The Incredible Hulk (1978 TV series)")*; *[Ironside](/wiki/Ironside_(1967_TV_series) "Ironside (1967 TV series)")*; *[The Rockford Files](/wiki/The_Rockford_Files "The Rockford Files")*;[[1]](#cite_note-TVGuide-1) *[Alias Smith and Jones](/wiki/Alias_Smith_and_Jones "Alias Smith and Jones")*;[[1]](#cite_note-TVGuide-1) *[Bonanza](/wiki/Bonanza "Bonanza")*; *[Kojak](/wiki/Kojak "Kojak")*; *[Gunsmoke](/wiki/Gunsmoke "Gunsmoke")*; *[Love, American Style](/wiki/Love,_American_Style "Love, American Style")*; *[Columbo: Lovely but Lethal](/wiki/List_of_Columbo_episodes#Season_3 "List of Columbo episodes")*; *[Cagney & Lacey](/wiki/Cagney_%26_Lacey "Cagney & Lacey")*; *[Adam-12](/wiki/Adam-12 "Adam-12")*; and *[Hawaii Five-O](/wiki/Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series) "Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series)")*.[[1]](#cite_note-TVGuide-1)

In 1974, she was the lead actress in the western *[Billy Two Hats](/wiki/Billy_Two_Hats "Billy Two Hats")*, alongside [Gregory Peck](/wiki/Gregory_Peck "Gregory Peck") and [Jack Warden](/wiki/Jack_Warden "Jack Warden").[[8]](#cite_note-8) In 1976, she portrayed [Anne Morrow Lindbergh](/wiki/Anne_Morrow_Lindbergh "Anne Morrow Lindbergh") in the television movie *[The Lindbergh Kidnapping Case](/wiki/The_Lindbergh_Kidnapping_Case "The Lindbergh Kidnapping Case")*, based on the real-life Lindbergh baby kidnapping/murder.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

A consistently busy actress through the 1970s, after giving birth to her daughter in 1982, Allen focused on family life and was only seen in a total of four acting appearances between 1980 and 1990. Allen's last role was in an episode of *[L.A. Law](/wiki/L.A._Law "L.A. Law")* in 1990, after which she retired from acting.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

Always interested in writing, Allen wrote a single episode of *[Baretta](/wiki/Baretta "Baretta")* in 1978 (her only writing credit for film or television). From the 1980s onward, and especially after retiring from acting, Allen concentrated on writing and publishing poetry and short fiction.

## Personal life and death

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sian_Barbara_Allen&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Personal life and death")]

Allen married Peter Burr Gelblum in 1979; they had a daughter, Emily. The marriage ended in 2001.[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12) She had two sisters, Mrs. Hannah Davie and [flash fiction](/wiki/Flash_fiction "Flash fiction") author, editor and teacher Meg Pokrass.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

Allen died from [Alzheimer's disease](/wiki/Alzheimer%27s_disease "Alzheimer's disease") in [Chapel Hill, North Carolina](/wiki/Chapel_Hill,_North_Carolina "Chapel Hill, North Carolina"), on March 31, 2025, at the age of 78.[[14]](#cite_note-14)
